sections.paragraph:
εὐσταθής, ές (s. prec. two entries; Hom. et al.; LXX; adv. εὐσταθῶς TestJob 36:6) stable, firm, calm τὸ εὐσταθές calmness, composure (Epict., Ench. 33, 11; Philo, Conf. Lingu. 43) MPol 7:2.—DELG s.v. στάθμη.

prec. prec. = preceding

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 413
